objective: Build and replay one batch-private historical-CAGR PEG computational slice for legacy capability #33 without freezing product semantics or activating the shared factor pipeline.
claim_ceiling: E1 batch-private historical-CAGR PEG kernel and deterministic fixture-replay evidence only; no approved product semantics, stable handoff, shared factor registration, real-source or shared-pipeline replay, Dagster asset, mart output, release activation, issue closure, or E2+ readiness claim

requirements: the queued S11 registration is merged to main before preparation
the preparation revision freezes the exact Local/CI corpus, activation base, and independent reviewer
each rung PR audits target-branch drift and updates only when it intersects declared paths or invalidates evidence
issue #284 contains the exact manifest path and SHA-256
issue #59 remains a freeze dependency and no provisional result may be represented as approved PEG semantics
no shared export, registry, base factor, lockfile, authoritative document, runtime binding, or release path changes

dependencies.artifact: approved historical-CAGR PEG formula, period, unit, availability, and negative-value semantics with product reference evidence

required_strata: valid positive price-to-earnings and positive historical CAGR
unavailable, future-known, stale, wrong-entity, and duplicate inputs
canonical input reordering and ambient Decimal-context determinism

release_activation.reason: S11 is an isolated provisional historical-CAGR PEG kernel; #59 semantic approval and the remaining #33 E2-E4 pipeline evidence are mandatory before shared use

negative_controls: future-known, unavailable, stale, wrong-entity, or duplicate input accepted
non-Decimal arithmetic, non-finite value, invalid period, or invalid unit accepted
zero or negative growth silently divided
negative earnings or nonpositive price-to-earnings represented as an ordinary positive PEG
input ordering or ambient Decimal context changes the result identity
analyst-consensus, guidance, vendor identity, source priority, shared registration, or release semantics introduced

invalidation: formula, annualization, period, unit, availability, negative-value policy, input identity, output identity, or corpus change creates a new batch revision
any need for a shared export, registry, base factor, contract, lockfile, authoritative document, or runtime binding blocks this batch until separately authorized
approval of #59 does not retroactively raise this provisional evidence ceiling or create a stable handoff

real-source, PIT-restatement, shared-pipeline replay, Dagster, mart, or release integration requires a separate higher-rung batch; deterministic replay of the frozen S11 fixture remains required for E1
rollback: Revert the isolated S11 batch package, tests, corpus, and evidence together; no accepted shared registry, runtime, mart, or release manifest may reference them.
